content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. The difference is that you do not remember all array elements correctly, and there are Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0. It is worth 60 points in Division 2, 3 and 4, and 100 points in Division 1.**

You have an array AAA of NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.

You can perform the following operation as many times as you want:


Choose L,RL, RL,R such that 1≤L<R≤N1 \le L < R \le N1≤L<R≤N and AL=ARA_L = A_RAL​=AR​, then update Ai:=ALA_i := A_LAi​:=AL​ for all L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R.
Let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the minimum number of distinct elements in the array AAA after applying operations optimally.

In this version, you have unfortunately forgotten some of the original elements of AAA. The original elements all satisfied 1≤Ai≤N1 \le A_i \le N1≤Ai​≤N, and if you have forgotten the original value, you are given Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 instead.

Find the minimum value of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all possible original arrays, i.e. minimum f(A)f(A)f(A) by optimally replacing the 000s in AAA with elements in [1,N][1, N][1,N].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible value of f(A)f(A)f(A), after optimal replacement.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are no 000s in the array to replace. We can choose L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 and operate to get [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1] which has only 111 distinct element.
Test case 222: Replace A2A_2A2​ with 222 to get [1,2,3,2][1, 2, 3, 2][1,2,3,2]. Then take L=2,R=4L = 2, R = 4L=2,R=4 and operate to get the array [1,2,2,2][1, 2, 2, 2][1,2,2,2] which has only 222 distinct elements.
